data.title: Cache webhook external_id to sync_product lookup

data.body:
Severity: performance medium. Source: TYB-12-followup performance audit.

Where: server/internal/httpserver/stripe_webhook.go:163-210.

What: webhook handler does two serial Printful round-trips on the request goroutine — GetSyncProductByExternalID (line 164) then CreateOrder (line 201). Stripe's webhook ack budget is ~10s; printful.DefaultTimeout is 30s per call. The sync_product was just created in /api/checkout/start seconds before the webhook fires.

Impact: a single slow Printful response causes Stripe to time out, mark the delivery failed, and retry up to 3 days later. The handler clears SeenSessions on 5xx so retries can succeed, but every hung delivery delays a real customer order by hours.

Recommendation: tiny LRU cache of (external_id -> sync_product) populated by /api/checkout/start when the sync_product is created. Webhook hits the cache first, falls back to the GET. Cache size 1024, TTL 24h. Effort: small.

Alternative: enqueue order placement to a background goroutine; ack webhook 200 immediately. Larger change, requires durable queue if the process can crash mid-order. Defer until needed.

Lowest-effort win on the same theme: pass a shorter ctx to the webhook's Printful calls (e.g., context.WithTimeout(ctx, 8*time.Second)) so a stuck call fails fast and Stripe retries quickly rather than burning the full 10s budget.

Pairs with TYB-17 (HTTP client tuning) which addresses the TLS-handshake overhead component.
